claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON drift repair and fail-closed invalid JSON handling as specified in the demand acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: JSON repair logic in parseJsonCandidate function handles Claude drift: line ranges, escaped backslashes, spacing around keys, trailing commas
evidence.why: This is the core implementation of minor recoverable JSON drift repair.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function attempts multiple parse strategies: direct parse, fenced code blocks, sliced object extraction, with error tracking
evidence.why: Shows multi-strategy JSON extraction with repair attempts and explicit error reporting.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, with blocked verdict and raw output excerpt
evidence.why: This is the fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable invalid JSON.

evidence.summary: Provider run JSON extraction with json_repaired flag tracking, passing parseError to normalization, and truncating output
evidence.why: Shows the runtime integration that records repair status and propagates parse errors.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift including: line 42-88 range format, escaped dot, double-space around key, markdown code block wrapper
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms drift repair works for real Claude output patterns.

evidence.summary: Test verifies invalid agent JSON returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocker code on all agent results and readiness blockers
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms fail-closed behavior with explicit blocker code.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses git ls-files for tracked files and walkFiles for full tree, with entry digest via hash/stat
evidence.why: Evidence of boundary detection implementation using Git-backed file tracking.

evidence.summary: Runtime creates boundary snapshot before agent loop, creates another after loop, diffs snapshots, and records violations
evidence.why: Evidence of complete boundary audit workflow around agent execution.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with violated status and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms boundary enforcement is independent of agent result success.

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED
evidence.why: Test evidence that confirms scope validation is enforced at normalization time.

risks: Malformed provider output could be silently accepted if parseJsonCandidate returns a false positive through the repair regexes
JSON repair regexes may not cover all future Claude output drift patterns
Boundary detection using git ls-files may miss untracked files in non-git repos

result.notes: All acceptance criteria verified: minor drift repair is implemented and tested, invalid JSON fails closed with explicit error code, readiness blocking is confirmed, and boundary enforcement is independent of agent success.
